sourceMovie.title: Woman of the Hour
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Woman of the Hour works as a crime movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Chinatown
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Woman of the Hour clicked for you, Chinatown (1974) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Woman of the Hour?
faq.answer: Try Chinatown, A Walk Among the Tombstones, The Last House on the Left—each shares DNA with Woman of the Hour while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Woman of the Hour?
faq.answer: Woman of the Hour (2024) is best described as Crime, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Woman of the Hour worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
